content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation, requests `requestWhenInUseAuthorization()`, calls `startUpdatingLocation()`, and stores GPS route data (`App/RunSessionManager.swift:1-15`), directly contradicted by the description.txt claim "maps every run with GPS."

Pierre: Apple's reviewers explicitly cross-check privacy policy claims against actual app behavior, and a direct contradiction like this ("we do not collect location" vs. active CoreLocation tracking used as a core feature) is a classic 5.1.1(i) rejection trigger. You need to update the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection and its purpose (route mapping), and ensure the App Privacy "Nutrition Labels" declare Location as collected data. Fix the policy text before submission — this will almost certainly be flagged in human review otherwise.
